name: Tofu Pecan Cutlets
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Brush a cookie sheet with oil.
3. Cut tofu in half lengthwise, then cut each half into 7 slices that are 1/2-inch thick and 1-1/2 inches square.
4. Marinate tofu in water, Tamari, Sherry, Sesame oil, Black pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon Basil for at least 30 minutes.
5. Mix 2 tablespoons Flour with 3/4 cup Water until well blended.
6. Mix together Bread crumbs, Pecans, Salt, Pepper, Garlic powder, and 1/4 teaspoon Basil.
7. Dip tofu into remaining Flour mixture, then into the Flour/Water mixture.
8. Dip tofu cutlets into the Bread crumb mixture and place on cookie sheet.
9. Bake for 10 minutes.
10. Turn cutlets over and bake an additional 10 minutes or until golden brown.
Per serving: 365 calories; 23g protein; 359mg sodium; 33g carbohydrates; 17g fat; 0mg cholesterol; 270mg calcium
Vegetarian Gourmet, August 1992/MM by Deanne
